summary: Reporter asks how to efficiently render 2048 colored spokes radiating from the center of a circle without moiré patterns, comparing DrawCircle-per-pixel (correct but slow) vs bitmap-per-spoke approach (fast but causes aliasing artifacts at edges).

codeSnippets: for (int k = 0; k < dataLength; k++) { surface.Canvas.DrawCircle((int)x, (int)y, 1f, paint); }
SKBitmap spokeBmp = new SKBitmap(new SKImageInfo((int)512, (int)1, ...)); canvas.DrawBitmap(spokeBmp, 0, 0);
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Blazor / SKPaintGLSurfaceEventArgs implied, no version specified

analysis.summary: The reporter is building a polar chart (radar/sweep display) with 2048 spokes, each 512 pixels long with per-pixel color data. Approach 1 (DrawCircle per pixel) is correct but incurs ~1M draw calls per frame. Approach 2 (1×512 bitmap + rotation) is fast but causes moiré because a single-pixel-wide line becomes sub-pixel at the circumference when many spokes are packed together. The correct solution is to render the spoke data directly into a full polar bitmap (using cartesian to polar conversion) or use DrawLine to draw each spoke as a single anti-aliased line with a gradient/color stepping pattern.
analysis.rationale: Title prefix '[QUESTION]' and body tone confirm this is a usage question, not a broken-behavior bug. No error or crash is described. The reporter explicitly asks for advice on technique. The API used (DrawCircle, SKBitmap, DrawBitmap, PeekPixels) all exist and work correctly.

keySignals.text: do any one have any idea or do i have to fall back to adress every pixel in the bitmap?
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter is asking for guidance on the right approach, not reporting a defect.

keySignals.text: This works no moire pattern but it is slow
keySignals.interpretation: Approach 1 (1M draw calls) is too slow; reporter needs a faster strategy.

keySignals.text: the problem is that because of a circles spoke get wider the longer away from center the pixel is
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter correctly identifies the aliasing root cause of Approach 2 — using single pixel bitmap without gap-filling.

codeInvestigation.finding: DrawLine(float x0, float y0, float x1, float y1, SKPaint paint) exists and is far cheaper than DrawCircle for this use case — one call per spoke instead of 512 calls; using StrokeWidth 1 with anti-alias draws a proper line segment.

GetPixelSpan() returns a Span<byte> backed by the bitmap's pixel buffer — reporter's Approach 2 uses this correctly for direct write; the moiré is not an API defect but a geometric gap when 2048 spokes of 1-pixel width cover a 3217-pixel circumference at r=512.

workarounds: Use canvas.DrawLine(midx, midy, x, y, paint) per spoke (2048 draw calls instead of 1M). Configure paint with StrokeWidth=1, IsAntialias=true, and set paint.Color per spoke. This is ~500x fewer draw calls than DrawCircle-per-pixel.

Pre-render all spokes into a 1024×1024 SKBitmap by directly writing pixels using cartesian-to-polar mapping (iterate each pixel of the bitmap, compute its angle+radius, look up the spoke and distance, write color). Then draw the bitmap once per frame. This avoids gaps at the circumference.
Use SKPath: for each spoke, path.MoveTo(center); path.LineTo(endPoint) and draw the full path with per-segment paints, or use an SKVertices sweep mesh.

resolution.hypothesis: Reporter wants a polar visualization with per-pixel color per spoke. The fundamental issue is that approach 2 (1-pixel bitmap + rotation) leaves gaps at large radii because angular step (~0.176°) at radius 512 spans >3 pixels of arc.

proposals.title: DrawLine per spoke
proposals.description: Replace DrawCircle-per-pixel loop with canvas.DrawLine(cx, cy, endX, endY, paint) per spoke. 2048 DrawLine calls is much faster than 1M DrawCircle calls. For per-pixel color control use an SKShader gradient on each paint.

proposals.title: Pre-render polar bitmap
proposals.description: Create a 1024×1024 bitmap. For each pixel, compute angle=atan2(y-cy, x-cx) and radius=sqrt((x-cx)^2+(y-cy)^2). Look up spoke index = (int)(angle/TWO_PI * 2048) and distance = (int)radius. Write the color from your spoke data. Draw the bitmap once per frame. Zero draw calls; perfect rendering.

resolution.recommendedReason: Eliminates moiré completely and reduces per-frame work to a single DrawBitmap call. For 2048 spokes updated at high frequency, the polar-bitmap approach scales well.

actionability.reason: This is a how-to usage question. The APIs work correctly. A clear answer with two alternative techniques can be provided and the issue closed as answered.

actions.description: Apply question and core area labels
Post answer with two recommended techniques for polar spoke rendering
Close as answered — usage question with clear solutions provided
